note: All values are approximate educational representations based on publicly available data (SpaceX, Rocket Lab, NASA, ULA). For educational use only.

families.name: SpaceX
families.tagline: Merlin & Raptor — From Reliability to Rapid Reusability
families.description: SpaceX utilises two main engine families, distinguished by fuel type and combustion cycle. Merlin (RP-1/LOX, gas-generator) powered Falcon 9 to become the world's most-flown rocket. Raptor (Methalox, full-flow staged combustion) operates at the highest chamber pressure of any production engine and is designed for deep-space reuse.
families.description_zh: SpaceX 使用兩大引擎家族：梅林（RP-1/液氧，燃氣發生器循環）使獵鷹9號成為世界飛行最多的火箭；猛禽（甲烷/液氧，全流量分級燃燒）是有史以來燃燒室壓力最高的量產引擎，專為深空任務設計。

engines.name: Merlin 1D
engines.key_feature: Highly reliable and reusable (200+ flights per engine). Powers both the first stage (×9) and the vacuum-optimised second stage (×1, vacuum variant). Single-engine-out capability provides additional reliability.
engines.key_feature_zh: 高度可靠且可重複使用（每台引擎超過200次飛行）。同時用於第一節（×9）和第二節（真空版×1）。單引擎失效備援能力提供額外可靠性。

educational.isp_note: SL Isp 282 s vs vacuum 311 s: the ~10% gap is caused by over-expanded exhaust at sea-level back-pressure. The nozzle expansion ratio of 16:1 is a compromise — larger would increase vacuum Isp but cause flow separation at sea level.
educational.cycle_note: Gas-generator cycle taps ~2% of propellant to drive the turbopumps. This small bleed reduces overall efficiency compared with staged-combustion engines.
educational.reuse_note: After MECO the first stage performs a boostback burn (~T+218 s), entry burn (~T+518 s), and landing burn (~T+607 s) — all using the same 9 Merlin 1D engines.

engines.name: Merlin 1D Vacuum
engines.key_feature: Extended nozzle (expansion ratio 165:1) maximises vacuum Isp at 380 s. The large carbon-composite nozzle skirt would cause separated flow and physical damage if fired at sea level.
engines.key_feature_zh: 延伸噴嘴（膨脹比 165:1）使真空比衝達到 380 秒。如在海平面點火，大型碳複合材料噴嘴裙會出現分離流並導致物理損壞。

educational.isp_note: Vacuum Isp of 380 s vs SL Merlin's 311 s — a 22% improvement achieved purely by expanding the nozzle from ratio 16 to 165. No change to combustion chemistry.
educational.design_trade: The nozzle adds ~20 kg and nearly doubles the engine length. For upper stages operating entirely in vacuum, this trade is always worthwhile.
educational.application: Used as the sole engine on Falcon 9's second stage; a restart capability enables multi-burn missions (e.g. Hohmann transfer orbits).

engines.name: Raptor 2
engines.key_feature: Highest chamber pressure (~300 bar) of any production rocket engine. Full-flow staged combustion routes all propellants through pre-burners, eliminating the efficiency penalty of a gas-generator. Designed for 1,000+ reuse cycles for Mars missions.
engines.key_feature_zh: 量產火箭引擎中最高燃燒室壓力（約 300 bar）。全流量分級燃燒將所有推進劑通過預燃室，消除燃氣發生器的效率損失。設計用於火星任務，目標重複使用 1,000+ 次。

educational.isp_note: Methane Isp (~327 s SL) is ~16% higher than RP-1 Merlin (~282 s SL). Methane also leaves less coking residue, enabling faster turnaround between flights.
educational.cycle_note: Full-flow staged combustion runs a fuel-rich pre-burner AND an oxidiser-rich pre-burner. Every molecule of propellant passes through a turbine before combustion — no bleed losses.
educational.pressure_note: At 300 bar, combustion is far more complete and the nozzle can be shorter for the same thrust. High pressure = more thrust per unit area = better T/W ratio.

engines.name: Raptor Vacuum
engines.key_feature: Extended carbon-composite nozzle optimised for deep-space vacuum. Six Raptor Vacuum engines power Starship's upper stage alongside three sea-level Raptors for atmospheric re-entry.
engines.key_feature_zh: 擴展型碳複合材料噴嘴，為深太空真空環境最佳化。六台猛禽真空版引擎為星艦上節提供動力，另配三台海平面版用於大氣層再入。

educational.isp_note: 382 s vacuum Isp vs Raptor 2 SL 363 s — a smaller gain than the Merlin SL-to-vacuum jump because FFSC already runs at very high efficiency.
educational.comparison: RS-25 still leads on vacuum Isp (452 s) because LH₂ has much higher exhaust velocity. But methane's higher density means less tank volume — crucial for vehicle packaging.

families.name: Rocket Lab
families.tagline: Rutherford — Electric-Pump Innovation for Small Satellites
families.description: Rocket Lab is known for its innovative approach to small satellite launches. The Rutherford engine uses battery-powered electric motors to drive propellant pumps — the first electric-pump-fed engine to reach orbit. This eliminates the gas-generator or pre-burner entirely, dramatically simplifying the propulsion system.
families.description_zh: Rocket Lab 以小衛星發射的創新方式著稱。盧瑟福引擎使用電池供電的電動馬達驅動推進劑泵——這是首個達到軌道的電動泵送引擎。這完全消除了燃氣發生器或預燃室，大大簡化了推進系統。

engines.name: Rutherford
engines.key_feature: First electric pump-fed engine to reach orbit. Battery-powered brushless DC motors drive the LOX and RP-1 pumps independently — no turbomachinery, no gas-generator losses, no pre-burner. Manufactured using 3-D printing in 24 hours.
engines.key_feature_zh: 首個達到軌道的電動泵送引擎。電池供電的無刷直流電機獨立驅動液氧和 RP-1 泵——無渦輪機械、無燃氣發生器損失、無預燃室。使用 3D 打印在 24 小時內製造。

educational.isp_note: Rutherford SL Isp 311 s is actually higher than Merlin 1D SL (282 s) despite the simple cycle. The advantage comes from 145 bar chamber pressure — well above Merlin's 97 bar.
educational.cycle_note: Electric pumping means zero turbopump bleed. The penalty is battery mass (~50 kg for a full burn), but this is small relative to propellant mass for short burns.
educational.scale_note: At 24.9 kN per engine × 9 engines = 224 kN total, Electron lifts a ~13,000 kg vehicle with ~300 kg payload — ideal for dedicated small-sat launches.

engines.name: Rutherford
engines.key_feature: Vacuum-optimised version with a larger nozzle. The single second-stage Rutherford enables precise orbit insertion and multiple restarts for payload deployment to different orbital planes.
engines.key_feature_zh: 配備較大噴嘴的真空最佳化版本。單台第二節盧瑟福引擎可實現精確入軌，多次重啟可將有效載荷部署到不同軌道面。

educational.restart_note: Multiple restarts are easy for electric pump-fed engines — just turn the motor back on. No turbopump spin-up delay or risk of rich combustion during restart.
educational.nozzle_note: Expanding the nozzle ratio from 11:1 (SL) to 17:1 (vacuum) gains ~32 s of Isp — modest because RP-1 exhaust is heavier than hydrogen, so nozzle gains are limited.

families.name: Legacy & Heavy Lift
families.tagline: RD-180, BE-4, RS-25 — High-Performance Heritage Engines
families.description: Major launch providers beyond SpaceX and Rocket Lab use different propellant combinations and combustion cycles, each optimised for specific mission profiles. The RD-180 demonstrates oxygen-rich staged combustion; the BE-4 is its US-made methane successor; and the RS-25 (SSME) holds the record for the highest Isp of any flight-proven hydrocarbon-free engine.
families.description_zh: SpaceX 和 Rocket Lab 以外的主要運載火箭提供商使用不同的推進劑組合和燃燒循環，各自針對特定任務剖面進行最佳化。RD-180 展示了富氧分級燃燒；BE-4 是其美製甲烷後繼者；RS-25（SSME）保持著所有飛行驗證引擎中最高比衝的紀錄。

engines.name: RD-180
engines.key_feature: Dual-combustion-chamber / dual-nozzle design in a single engine assembly. Oxygen-rich staged combustion achieves very high chamber pressure (257 bar) with a single turbopump set. Notable for its very high SL Isp of 311 s — matching Rutherford despite the much larger scale.
engines.key_feature_zh: 單一引擎組件中的雙燃燒室/雙噴嘴設計。富氧分級燃燒以單一渦輪泵組達到很高的燃燒室壓力（257 bar）。其 311 s 的海平面比衝與盧瑟福相當——儘管尺寸大得多。

educational.isp_note: RD-180 SL Isp of 311 s vs Merlin 282 s — staged combustion's advantage is clear. More of the propellant's energy goes to thrust, less is wasted in the turbopump bleed stream.
educational.cycle_note: Oxygen-rich staged combustion is difficult because hot, oxygen-rich gas is highly corrosive to metal. Russian engineers solved this with special alloys; US engineers initially avoided it.
educational.geopolitics: The RD-180 was supplied by NPO Energomash (Russia). US sanctions following 2014 led ULA to develop the BE-4 as a domestic replacement.

engines.name: BE-4
engines.key_feature: US-designed methane alternative to the Russian RD-180. Uses oxygen-rich staged combustion with methane — a combination that avoids RP-1 coking and enables reusability. Powers both Blue Origin's New Glenn and ULA's Vulcan Centaur.
engines.key_feature_zh: 俄制 RD-180 的美製甲烷替代品。使用甲烷的富氧分級燃燒——這種組合避免了 RP-1 結焦並實現重複使用性。為 Blue Origin 的新格倫號和 ULA 的火神半人馬座提供動力。

educational.isp_note: BE-4 SL Isp ~321 s sits between RD-180 (311 s) and Raptor 2 (327 s). Methane's lower carbon content reduces soot build-up compared to RP-1, simplifying engine inspection between flights.
educational.methane_advantage: Methane can be synthesised from CO₂ and water using electricity ('Sabatier reaction'). This is why both SpaceX and Blue Origin chose it for reusable/Mars-oriented engines.
educational.reuse_note: New Glenn's BE-4 booster is designed for reuse similarly to Falcon 9 — landing on a drone ship after separation.

engines.name: RS-25 (SSME)
engines.rocket: NASA Space Launch System (SLS) Core Stage
engines.key_feature: Highest vacuum Isp (452 s) of any flight-proven engine, achieved through the very low molecular weight of hydrogen exhaust. Originally the Space Shuttle Main Engine; now used expendably on SLS. Known for extraordinary mechanical complexity — it has been called the most complex machine ever built.
engines.key_feature_zh: 所有飛行驗證引擎中最高的真空比衝（452 s），通過氫排氣的極低分子量實現。最初是太空梭主引擎；現在以一次性方式用於 SLS。以其非凡的機械複雜性著稱——被稱為有史以來製造的最複雜機器。

educational.isp_note: 452 s vacuum Isp vs RP-1 Merlin Vacuum's 380 s — a 19% advantage from hydrogen's exhaust velocity. Hydrogen exhaust (H₂O) has molecular weight ~18; RP-1 exhaust (~CO₂+H₂O) is ~22. Lower MW = higher exhaust velocity = higher Isp.
educational.hydrogen_trade: LH₂ density is only ~71 kg/m³ (vs RP-1's 820 kg/m³). Huge tanks are needed for the same propellant mass. SLS core stage is 8.4 m diameter to accommodate them.
educational.cost_note: Each RS-25 costs ~$146 M (2023, SLS contract). By comparison, Merlin cost is estimated at ~$1 M. The RS-25 demonstrates that extreme performance has extreme cost — a key trade for students to explore.
educational.history: The Space Shuttle flew 135 missions (1981–2011) using three RS-25s each. 16 refurbished Shuttle engines are being used on SLS; when they run out, new RS-25s will be manufactured at higher cost.
